page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 8 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 31.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

As a dedicated resource instance, the c4d-standard-8 allocates full CPU cores to workloads, avoiding the performance degradation associated with shared-core environments. The balanced ratio of compute to memory positions this server as a standard general-purpose resource. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of bundled local storage and GPU accelerators keeps the resource profile streamlined, allowing users to pay strictly for compute and memory capacity while attaching external storage as needed.

This server configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads that require dedicated CPU performance without massive memory footprints or local storage requirements. Suitable applications include web serving, application servers, microservices, containerized deployments, and moderate database workloads that rely on network-attached storage.
